title: panctenis pedicularia
side_effects: Panctenis pedicularia can have side effects if used improperly, such as diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. If ingested in large quantities, it can lead to vomiting, headaches, and dizziness. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause damage to the liver and kidneys. Overuse of Panctenis pedicularia for medicinal purposes can also interact with other medications and exacerbate pre-existing health conditions.
